name: coverage_iter
description: One verilator coverage iteration — build, run, merge, report, gaps, close, re-verify

tasks.content: Build DUT with verilator + coverage flags
tasks.detail: Run /coverage-build {dut}. Confirms verilator is installed, filelist exists, and the DUT compiles cleanly with --coverage-line --coverage-toggle. Output: build_{dut}_cov/ with C++ sources. If RTL has verilator-incompatible constructs (inline #delays, force/release, cross-module refs), report them as TODO escalation items rather than silently skipping.

tasks.content: Run all existing testcases under verilator backend
tasks.detail: Run the existing cocotb regression with SIM=verilator (e.g. cd {dut}/cocotb && make SIM=verilator MODULE=tests.tb). Capture coverage.dat per test if running multiple modules. Confirm regression PASSES (results.xml has no <failure>) — coverage on a failed run is meaningless and must be flagged immediately.

Run /coverage-merge {dut}. Verifies all generated .dat files are found and merges them into {dut}/cov/merged.dat. If 0 .dat files found, the regression step did not actually instrument — debug the build/run flow before continuing.

tasks.content: Generate annotated source + LCOV info report
tasks.detail: Run /coverage-report {dut}. Produces {dut}/cov/annotated/ (per-source-file .cov annotations with hit counts), {dut}/cov/coverage.info (LCOV format), {dut}/cov/coverage.json (SSOT-aligned summary), and {dut}/sim/coverage_report.md. Records the line% summary printed at the end. Compare to the previous iteration's metrics if available — log the delta via todo_note. If SSOT branch/FSM metrics cannot be measured by the generic flow, keep them as limitations/escalations instead of adding an IP-specific workaround script.

Run /coverage-gaps {dut} 10. Read the top hits AND open the relevant {dut}/cov/annotated/<file>.cov files to see context around each unhit line. Group by module and decide which gaps are: (a) reachable but missing test, (b) unreachable / dead code → waiver, (c) configuration-dependent (parameterized off in this build).

tasks.content: Add directed test for highest-priority gap
tasks.detail: Pick the gap with the highest impact (most lines covered per added test) from the previous step. Write a new cocotb test (or extend tb.py) that exercises the missing path. Quote the file:line evidence from annotated/ in the implementation comment so the next reviewer can verify the targeting was correct.

tasks.content: Re-run regression to confirm coverage delta
tasks.detail: Re-run the full regression under SIM=verilator including the new test. Run /coverage-merge + /coverage-report. Compare new line% / toggle% to baseline. ACCEPT only if: (1) regression still passes (no <failure>), (2) coverage actually increased, (3) the previously-uncovered lines now show non-zero hits in annotated/.

tasks.content: Decide: continue iterating or write waivers for unreachable gaps
tasks.detail: If line% < 95% or toggle% < 80% AND there are reachable gaps remaining → start another iter. If only unreachable / configuration-locked code remains → write {dut}/cov/.cov_waiver entries with file:line + reason for each, then exit. Waiver decisions must cite specific evidence from the RTL (e.g. 'gpio_core.sv:142 — TIE_LOW parameter forces this branch dead').
